_comment_top: OTGW-firmware lint policy (adr-kit v0.9.0+). ADRs 1 through 81 predate adoption of the four canonical adr-kit gates and use a legacy structure (Decision drivers, Pros and Cons, Risks and Mitigations subsections). They are linted in advisory mode for completeness/evidence/clarity. ADRs 82 onward are linted strictly, so any new ADR is held to the canonical-seven section list out of the box. Consistency stays always_strict regardless of strict_from: filename / heading mismatches and duplicate ADR numbers are real bugs at any age.
_comment_template_choice: We deliberately do NOT override template.required_sections here. The recent ADRs (082+) currently lack a few canonical headings (Status as heading, Related Decisions, References); the FAIL output is the actionable next step. If at some point we adopt a project-specific template (for example: only ## Context / ## Decision / ## Consequences as load-bearing headings, with Status inline as a bold prefix), add a template.required_sections array here listing the project's actual required headings, and the FAILs will resolve.
_comment_judge: pre_commit_timeout_ms is the budget for the non-blocking perf WARN only. The LLM pass (Sonnet over all llm_judge:true ADRs) legitimately takes ~30s; allow up to 45s so a thorough pass does not trip a spurious warning on every commit. The hard LLM call timeout stays at the adr-kit default (judge.llm_timeout_seconds, 120s) and is not the bottleneck.
